 {"id":"bd-114p","title":"demo_showcase: advanced interactions (mouse/resize/focus/paste)","description":"Make the demo feel premium by fully supporting advanced terminal interactions:
- mouse (click/scroll)
- resize responsiveness
- focus/blur awareness
- bracketed paste

Why:
- These are the “paper cuts” that distinguish toy TUIs from production ones.
- Supporting them forces correctness in the bubbletea event loop and component integration.

Must support (minimum):
- Mouse:
  - enable mouse mode in the Program
  - scroll wheel maps naturally to focused viewport/list/table
  - clicks focus/select (and enable obvious affordances on clickable UI)
- Resize:
  - handle resize events and recompute layout
  - preserve selection/scroll positions as best-effort
- Focus:
  - clearly visible focus state (inputs, query bars, panes)
  - consistent focus traversal
- Bracketed paste:
  - paste into search bars/forms without exploding into per-keystroke noise
  - “paste-aware” update logic (treat as single input chunk)

Acceptance:
- Mouse scroll works in viewports/lists/tables without fighting keyboard navigation.
- Resize reflows the UI and keeps it usable.
- Focus state is obvious everywhere.
- Paste works in search/forms and is visibly robust.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - focus traversal rules
  - bracketed paste chunk handling
  - resize layout breakpoints (given width/height)
- E2E:
  - docs scenario validates resize reflow
  - where feasible, add E2E injection for mouse wheel sequences (xterm) to prove scroll mapping.

Notes:
- Always degrade gracefully in headless/CI environments.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:09.027017204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:43:10.181833548Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-114p","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:09.040330682Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-11c9","title":"Add bubbletea ReleaseTerminal/RestoreTerminal lifecycle methods","description":"Add Bubbletea lifecycle methods ReleaseTerminal/RestoreTerminal to mirror Go API.

Scope:
- Define public API methods on Program (and async variant if needed).
- Ensure terminal state transitions are safe (raw mode, alternate screen, cursor visibility).
- Integrate with existing lifecycle hooks and ensure idempotency.
- Add unit tests for state transitions and error handling.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- ReleaseTerminal and RestoreTerminal are exposed and documented.
- Unit tests verify terminal mode transitions and idempotent behavior.
- Conformance/integration tests updated if Go has fixtures for these lifecycle calls.","notes":"Files: crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs, crates/bubbletea/src/terminal.rs (if present), tests/conformance/crates/bubbletea/mod.rs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:31.118149978Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:32:38.763706377Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T01:32:38.763653767Z","close_reason":"Implemented release_terminal() and restore_terminal() commands in bubbletea::screen. Added ReleaseTerminalMsg and RestoreTerminalMsg. Both sync and async event loops handle these messages to temporarily release/restore terminal control for subprocess execution.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-13np","title":"demo_showcase core: bootstrap App from Config (seed/theme/toggles)","description":"Implement the single, canonical bootstrap path from `demo_showcase::Config` to a fully initialized `App` model.

Why:
- Prevents scattered initialization logic.
- Makes headless testing and `--self-check` deterministic.

Deliverables:
- `App::new(config: Config) -> App` (or similar) that:
  - initializes theme context/preset
  - initializes domain data from seed
  - initializes page/router state
  - applies toggles (mouse/animations/no-color/no-alt-screen)

Acceptance:
- All entrypoints (interactive, self-check, ssh mode) use this same bootstrap.
- Bootstrapping is unit-testable.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:06.629120905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:24.848645834Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:20.218711349Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-3bz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:21.491726389Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:06.642870498Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:23.046165231Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:24.848608444Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:18.934928904Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-14v4","title":"demo_showcase core: navigation sidebar (List + filtering)","description":"Build a polished navigation sidebar that makes the multi-page app feel cohesive.

Why:
- Navigation is constant UI; if it feels clunky, the whole app feels clunky.
- It’s also a great place to showcase bubbles list + filtering.

Requirements:
- Nav list:
  - use `bubbles::list` (filterable) or a custom list if needed
  - entries for Dashboard/Jobs/Docs/Wizard/Files/Logs/Settings
  - clear selected/current page highlight
- Filtering:
  - quick filter input that narrows nav items
  - escape clears filter and restores full list
- Mouse (optional):
  - click to navigate

Integration:
- Navigation emits a single clean app-level route message (no page-to-page coupling).

Acceptance:
- Switching pages feels instant.
- Filtering nav demonstrates textinput+list integration and feels fast.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:58.826538621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:32:56.079381744Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14v4","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:19.445789703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-14v4","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:18.267533574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-14v4","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:58.851229842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-15ip","title":"glamour: Handle extreme table width edge cases gracefully","description":"# Task: Handle Extreme Table Width Edge Cases

## Problem Statement
In table.rs, the width calculation can produce degenerate results when
max_table_width is very small:

```rust
// Lines 505-550
let total_padding = column_count * config.cell_padding * 2;
let total_borders = (column_count + 1) * config.border_width;
let fixed_overhead = total_padding + total_borders;
let available_content = config.max_table_width.saturating_sub(fixed_overhead);
```

When max_table_width < fixed_overhead:
- available_content = 0
- All columns get min_width or 0
- Table becomes unreadable or panics on render

## Example Scenarios
1. Terminal width: 20, Table columns: 10, Padding: 1, Border: 1
   - fixed_overhead = 10*2 + 11 = 31
   - available_content = 20 - 31 = 0 (saturated)
   - Each column gets 0 width

2. min_width: 3, columns: 100, max_width: 50
   - Required: 300 chars minimum
   - Available: 50 chars
   - Result: Severely truncated, potentially invalid

## Solution Design

### Validation and Fallback
```rust
fn calculate_column_widths(
    table: &Table,
    config: &TableConfig,
) -> Result<Vec<usize>, TableError> {
    let column_count = table.column_count();
    let fixed_overhead = calculate_overhead(column_count, config);
    
    // Check if table can fit at all
    if config.max_table_width > 0 && config.max_table_width < fixed_overhead {
        // Table overhead alone exceeds max width
        // Options:
        // 1. Return error
        // 2. Reduce padding/borders
        // 3. Use minimum viable rendering
        return Err(TableError::InsufficientWidth {
            required: fixed_overhead,
            available: config.max_table_width,
        });
    }
    
    let available_content = config.max_table_width.saturating_sub(fixed_overhead);
    let min_required = column_count * config.min_column_width;
    
    if available_content < min_required {
        // Not enough space for minimum columns
        // Reduce to fit or error
        tracing::warn!(
            "Table content requires {} chars but only {} available",
            min_required,
            available_content
        );
    }
    
    // Continue with normal calculation...
}
```

### Graceful Degradation Options
1. **Reduce padding**: Set padding to 0 when space is tight
2. **Remove borders**: Use borderless style when constrained
3. **Horizontal scroll**: Indicate content continues (not visible)
4. **Column subset**: Only show first N columns that fit
5. **Wrap content**: Allow multi-line cells

## Implementation Steps
1. Add validation at start of width calculation
2. Define minimum viable table (1 char per column)
3. Add fallback rendering mode for tight spaces
4. Log warnings for degraded rendering
5. Add tests for edge cases

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn table_handles_narrow_width() {
    let table = Table::new(vec![
        vec!["Col1", "Col2", "Col3"],
        vec!["Data", "Data", "Data"],
    ]);
    
    let config = TableConfig {
        max_table_width: 10,  // Very narrow!
        ..Default::default()
    };
    
    // Should not panic
    let result = render_table(&table, &config);
    
    // Should produce some output (possibly degraded)
    assert!(!result.is_empty());
}

#[test]
fn table_width_zero() {
    let table = Table::new(vec![vec!["A"]]);
    let config = TableConfig {
        max_table_width: 0,  // No limit
        ..Default::default()
    };
    
    let result = render_table(&table, &config);
    assert!(result.contains("A"));
}

#[test]
fn table_single_column_narrow() {
    let table = Table::new(vec![vec!["LongCellContent"]]);
    let config = TableConfig {
        max_table_width: 5,
        min_column_width: 1,
        ..Default::default()
    };
    
    let result = render_table(&table, &config);
    // Should truncate with ellipsis
    assert!(result.contains("…"));
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No panic with any max_table_width value
- [ ] Graceful degradation for narrow widths
- [ ] Warning logged for degraded rendering
- [ ] Tests cover width=0, width<overhead, width<min scenarios
- [ ] Documentation explains behavior for edge cases","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:22:39.261784345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:22:39.345406214Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edge-case","glamour","robustness","table"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15ip","depends_on_id":"bd-8wlh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:22:39.345365178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-15xi","title":"Logs: copy/export + clear actions","description":"High-polish actions for the Logs page.

Why:
- Real ops tools treat logs as data you can *use* (export, copy, inspect), not just a scrolling wall.
- This is also a great way to demo bubbletea effect composition (batch/sequence) and "real product" ergonomics.

Actions to implement:
- Copy:
  - copy current selected line (if selection exists)
  - copy visible window (current viewport slice)
  - if clipboard integration is not available in all environments, fall back to writing to a file and show the path
- Export:
  - export the full log buffer to a file
  - output location should be deterministic/test-friendly:
    - in E2E/self-check mode: write under `target/demo_showcase_e2e/` (or a Config-provided artifacts dir)
    - otherwise: write to a sensible user-facing path (e.g., `./demo_showcase_exports/`)
  - prefer a stable plain-text format; optionally provide a richer styled export if feasible
- Inspect in pager (stretch):
  - open the exported log file in `$PAGER` using the shell-out flow (release terminal -> run -> restore)
- Clear:
  - clear the buffer (with a confirm if needed) and show a toast

Implementation guidance:
- Use `bubbletea::batch(...)` for "toast + log entry + state update".
- Use the shared shell-out action for pager integration (no ad-hoc terminal hacks).
- All failures must become toasts/log entries, never panics.

Acceptance:
- Actions are fast, obvious, and feel like a real tool.
- No terminal artifacts (cursor/raw/alt-screen) after running actions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:41.355134177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:43.437949348Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logs","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-194c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:08:07.823273731Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:43.437920845Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:08:09.126120827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-2qx1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:41.394965242Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-9zov","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:46.950082977Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15xi","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:44.337497742Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2kp1","title":"Settings: about/credits + diagnostics","description":"Add a polished About + Diagnostics section inside Settings.

Why:
- This is *real product* behavior (every serious ops tool has an About/Diagnostics panel).
- It doubles as a developer-facing debug surface and a user-facing confidence signal.

Diagnostics content (at minimum):
- demo_showcase version + build info (crate versions for bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour/huh/harmonica/wish/charmed_log)
- active runtime Config (theme name, seed, toggles like mouse/animations/no-color/no-alt-screen)
- terminal info (detected color profile, TERM/COLORTERM, width/height last known)

Nice-to-have (if available cheaply):
- feature flags enabled (async, ssh, syntax)
- recent error/notification summary

Actions:
- "Copy diagnostics" (to clipboard if feasible, otherwise export to file)
- "Open diagnostics in pager" using the shell-out flow (bubbletea release/restore + sequence)

Acceptance:
- Diagnostics are readable, well-formatted, and update when Config/theme changes.
- Actions never hang in CI/headless mode; failures become toasts/logs.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:09.931592707Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:46.057423922Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","settings"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-194c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:58.531010722Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:46.057393756Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-274o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:10.201659035Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:56.044885213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:57.301987137Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:13.453221221Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2lkz","title":"Docs: in-doc search + match navigation","description":"Add search within the current Docs page (like a pager/reader).

Why:
- Search is a make-or-break capability for terminal docs viewers.
- It showcases textinput + highlight rendering + keybinding conventions.

Requirements:
- `/` enters search mode (focuses a query input).
- Highlights matches in the rendered doc output.
- `n`/`N` jumps to next/previous match.
- Show match count and current match index (e.g., "3/12").

Performance:
- Do not require an expensive full markdown re-render on every keystroke.
- Prefer searching within the already-rendered text buffer and applying highlight overlays.

Testability:
- The search/match navigation logic should be unit tested via the shared filtering/search engine tests.
- Covered by the Docs E2E scenario.

Acceptance:
- Feels like a real pager: fast, predictable, keyboard-centric.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:44.495821283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:23.489705254Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:44.511693330Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:33.889650563Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-3jg2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:31.319028559Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-i0i9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:28.626427229Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2mvl","title":"Quality: snapshot tests for key views","description":"Add snapshot-style tests for key rendered views so UI regressions are caught early.

Why:
- TUIs are easy to break visually (alignment, borders, truncation) with small refactors.
- Snapshots complement state-based tests by asserting the actual rendered output.

What to snapshot (minimum):
- Dashboard (80x24)
- Jobs list/table (80x24)
- Logs (a few entries, follow mode on/off)
- Docs (one doc rendered at two widths)
- Settings (toggles + theme picker)

Snapshot strategy:
- Prefer ASCII/no-color snapshots for stability.
- If we snapshot colored output, normalize to deterministic sequences or snapshot via the "export plain" path.
- Keep snapshots small and focused (don’t snapshot megabytes of logs).

Acceptance:
- Snapshots are deterministic in CI.
- Failures are diff-friendly and point to the exact view that changed.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:28.393184434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:30:23.677208502Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","insta","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:28.411924655Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:21.323882037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:18.666497689Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:23.910513817Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2myg","title":"Dashboard: live metrics + status indicators","description":"Wire dashboard widgets to the demo data model so the dashboard is *alive* (real-time updates, plausible trends, state changes).

Why:
- Static numbers look fake. Live metrics make the dashboard feel like a real ops tool.
- This also validates the simulation engine and the app’s tick/update architecture.

Requirements (minimum):
- Live-updating metrics:
  - requests/sec (or throughput)
  - p95 latency (or similar)
  - error rate
  - job throughput / queue depth
- Health/status indicators:
  - per-metric health state (ok/warn/error) derived from thresholds
  - trends (up/down/flat) based on deltas over a window
  - at least one periodic “incident” / degradation to prove the UI can surface issues
- Feedback:
  - state transitions produce a toast/alert + (optional) log entry
  - drill-down panel should explain *why* a metric is warn/error (thresholds, recent values)

Implementation notes:
- Metrics come from the deterministic simulation engine; use a seed in test/E2E.
- Avoid noisy/flappy states:
  - consider hysteresis or smoothing so warn/error doesn’t flicker on boundaries.

Acceptance:
- Values visibly change over time.
- Health transitions are obvious and explainable.
- No flicker/jank; dashboard remains responsive.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - health state computation (thresholds + hysteresis if used)
  - trend computation over a known sequence
  - “incident” generation is deterministic for a fixed seed
- E2E:
  - smoke tour sees changing values
  - settings/theme scenario confirms dashboard updates immediately after toggles.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:57.979377768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:47:27.312422003Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:47:27.312380976Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:57.995055004Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:16.935347602Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:18.142227251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2myg","title":"Dashboard: live metrics + status indicators","description":"Wire dashboard widgets to the demo data model so the dashboard is *alive* (real-time updates, plausible trends, state changes).

Why:
- Static numbers look fake. Live metrics make the dashboard feel like a real ops tool.
- This also validates the simulation engine and the app’s tick/update architecture.

Requirements (minimum):
- Live-updating metrics:
  - requests/sec (or throughput)
  - p95 latency (or similar)
  - error rate
  - job throughput / queue depth
- Health/status indicators:
  - per-metric health state (ok/warn/error) derived from thresholds
  - trends (up/down/flat) based on deltas over a window
  - at least one periodic “incident” / degradation to prove the UI can surface issues
- Feedback:
  - state transitions produce a toast/alert + (optional) log entry
  - drill-down panel should explain *why* a metric is warn/error (thresholds, recent values)

Implementation notes:
- Metrics come from the deterministic simulation engine; use a seed in test/E2E.
- Avoid noisy/flappy states:
  - consider hysteresis or smoothing so warn/error doesn’t flicker on boundaries.

Acceptance:
- Values visibly change over time.
- Health transitions are obvious and explainable.
- No flicker/jank; dashboard remains responsive.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - health state computation (thresholds + hysteresis if used)
  - trend computation over a known sequence
  - “incident” generation is deterministic for a fixed seed
- E2E:
  - smoke tour sees changing values
  - settings/theme scenario confirms dashboard updates immediately after toggles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:57.979377768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:38.344449577Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:38.344380729Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:47:27.312380976Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:57.995055004Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:16.935347602Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:18.142227251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw3","title":"lipgloss: Code Consolidation & ANSI Parsing Hardening","description":"# Epic: lipgloss Code Consolidation & ANSI Parsing Hardening

## Background
The lipgloss crate provides terminal styling (colors, borders, layout). During deep review,
code duplication and incomplete ANSI handling were identified as maintenance risks.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Three Independent visible_width() Implementations - HIGH
**Locations**:
- lib.rs lines 365-417: Full state machine with Normal, Esc, Csi, Osc states
- style.rs lines 1965-1996: Simplified two-state approach
- backend.rs lines 621-640: Even more simplified, no OSC handling

**Problem**: Each version has different completeness levels. They may produce different
results for the same input, causing layout inconsistencies.

**Risk**: Maintenance nightmare - bug fixes need to be applied to 3 places.

### 2. Incomplete OSC Sequence Handling - MEDIUM
**Location**: lib.rs lines 403-411
**Problem**: OSC sequence termination (ST = ESC \) is not properly validated.
The state machine transitions to Esc state on seeing ESC but doesn't verify
the following backslash character.

**Risk**: Malformed OSC sequences could cause incorrect width calculations.

### 3. Border Color Indexing Confusion - LOW
**Problem**: Border colors stored as [top, right, bottom, left] but accessed
inconsistently across different methods.

**Risk**: Wrong colors could be applied to wrong edges in edge cases.

## Design Philosophy
- Single Source of Truth: One canonical visible_width implementation
- Defensive Parsing: Handle all edge cases in ANSI parsing
- Clear Documentation: Document the expected format for all color arrays

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single visible_width() function used everywhere
- [ ] Complete ANSI escape sequence handling including OSC
- [ ] Consistent border color indexing throughout
- [ ] All changes have regression tests","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:16:49.723527312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:16:49.747276437Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ansi-parsing","code-quality","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw3","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:16:49.747238416Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nym","title":"Fix wish e2e tests: require multi_thread tokio runtime","description":"The wish e2e tests are failing with 'can call blocking only when running on the multi-threaded runtime'. The lib.rs uses tokio::task::block_in_place which requires flavor=multi_thread, but tests use #[tokio::test] which defaults to current_thread. Fix by changing all #[tokio::test] to #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread")] in the wish e2e test files.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:28:17.908864434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:29:50.398369543Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:29:50.398308128Z","close_reason":"Fixed all 13 #[tokio::test] attributes in wish e2e tests to use flavor=multi_thread for block_in_place compatibility.  {"id":"bd-2oku","title":"Unit tests: theme switching invariants","description":"Add unit tests for the demo_showcase theme system.

Why:
- The showcase leans heavily on visuals; theme switching bugs are obvious and embarrassing.
- Theme behavior must be deterministic for snapshot/E2E artifacts.

Coverage:
- Preset switching:
  - switching themes updates semantic tokens (primary/success/warn/error/muted, etc.)
  - key shared helpers (`card`, `panel`, `badge`, `table_style`, etc.) produce different output as expected
- No-color / ASCII mode:
  - output contains no ANSI escapes
  - layout still uses spacing/borders appropriately
- Contrast checks (if implemented):
  - contrast validation rejects clearly invalid combinations OR emits a warning and falls back

Acceptance:
- Theme changes never leave mixed styles (no partially-updated output).
- Tests are deterministic and do not require a TTY.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:07.857613637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:29:39.663359757Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oku","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:07.873808155Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2oku","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:21.604530629Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2oku","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:24.186443099Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2oku","depends_on_id":"bd-k52c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:22.895595502Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
